title: Spanish-American War
description: Test your knowledge on the Spanish-American War!

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d6a0ace506ef5faafec8d90ab276be02.jpg)

>-Source: Louis Dalrymple, "The duty of the hour -- to save her not only from Spain but from a worse fate," [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Out_of_the_frying_pan.jpg) 1898

**The image most directly illustrates a United States foreign policy that emphasized which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: intervening in Latin American affairs after potential misgovernment by European powers
abstaining from conflict with other countries to maintain isolationism
spreading Christianity to countries where Christianity wasn’t commonly practiced
maintaining strong alliances with European countries to have access to foreign markets

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d6a0ace506ef5faafec8d90ab276be02.jpg)

>-Source: Louis Dalrymple, "The duty of the hour -- to save her not only from Spain but from a worse fate," [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Out_of_the_frying_pan.jpg) 1898

**Which of the following United States actions taken after the Spanish-American War most directly reflects a continuation of the concerns expressed in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: suppressing a Filipino rebellion against increased control by a foreign government
annexing Hawaii and Puerto Rico into the United States after the Spanish-American War
adopting a policy of containment to reduce European colonization around the world
creating a policy that allowed Cuban refugees to become permanent residents after living in the US for a year

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d6a0ace506ef5faafec8d90ab276be02.jpg)

>-Source: Louis Dalrymple, "The duty of the hour -- to save her not only from Spain but from a worse fate," [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Out_of_the_frying_pan.jpg) 1898

**The cartoon was intended to do which of the following?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: persuade the United States to fight the Spanish on behalf of the Cubans
promote an agenda to Christianize the Cubans after Spanish colonization
advocate for the end of the Spanish-American War
convince the United States to purchase Cuba from the Spanish

assessment_items.question_content:
>*NAVAL OFFICERS THINK THE MAINE WAS DESTROYED BY A SPANISH MINE.*

> "George Bryson, the Journal’s special reporter at Havana, writes that it is the secret opinion of many people in Havana that the war ship *Maine* was destroyed by a mine and 258 men were killed on purpose by the Spanish. This is the opinion of several American naval authorities. The Spaniards, it is believed, arranged to have *the Maine* drop anchor over a harbor mine. Wires connected the mine to the magazine of the ship. If this is true, the brutal nature of the Spaniards will be shown by the fact that they waited to explode the mine until all the men had gone to sleep. Spanish officials are protesting too much that they did not do it. Our government has ordered an investigation. This newspaper has sent divers to Havana to report on the condition of the wreck. This newspaper is also offering a \$50,000 reward for exclusive evidence that will convict whoever is responsible. "

>-Source: *New York Journal and Advertiser,* 1898

**The ideas expressed in the excerpt contributed most directly to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: public support for the United States to declare war on the Spanish
public outcry for a return to isolationism and an end to the Spanish-American War
The president issuing a statement calling for an end to unrestricted submarine warfare
The president calling for the internment of Spanish-American people in the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
>*NAVAL OFFICERS THINK THE MAINE WAS DESTROYED BY A SPANISH MINE.*

> "George Bryson, the Journal’s special reporter at Havana, writes that it is the secret opinion of many people in Havana that the war ship *Maine* was destroyed by a mine and 258 men were killed on purpose by the Spanish. This is the opinion of several American naval authorities. The Spaniards, it is believed, arranged to have *the Maine* drop anchor over a harbor mine. Wires connected the mine to the magazine of the ship. If this is true, the brutal nature of the Spaniards will be shown by the fact that they waited to explode the mine until all the men had gone to sleep. Spanish officials are protesting too much that they did not do it. Our government has ordered an investigation. This newspaper has sent divers to Havana to report on the condition of the wreck. This newspaper is also offering a \$50,000 reward for exclusive evidence that will convict whoever is responsible. "

>-Source: *New York Journal and Advertiser,* 1898

**The events described in the excerpt contributed most directly to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Spanish-American War
Mexican-American War
World War I
Philippine-American War

assessment_items.question_content:
>*NAVAL OFFICERS THINK THE MAINE WAS DESTROYED BY A SPANISH MINE.*

> "George Bryson, the Journal’s special reporter at Havana, writes that it is the secret opinion of many people in Havana that the war ship *Maine* was destroyed by a mine and 258 men were killed on purpose by the Spanish. This is the opinion of several American naval authorities. The Spaniards, it is believed, arranged to have *the Maine* drop anchor over a harbor mine. Wires connected the mine to the magazine of the ship. If this is true, the brutal nature of the Spaniards will be shown by the fact that they waited to explode the mine until all the men had gone to sleep. Spanish officials are protesting too much that they did not do it. Our government has ordered an investigation. This newspaper has sent divers to Havana to report on the condition of the wreck. This newspaper is also offering a \$50,000 reward for exclusive evidence that will convict whoever is responsible. "

>-Source: *New York Journal and Advertiser,* 1898

**The tactics used by this excerpt best serve as an example of which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yellow journalism
Muckraking
Expansionism
Americanization

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/95f81ad4aa2c4556be57cfcd0f330121.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1899BalanceCartoon.jpg), 1899

**The image most directly illustrates a United States foreign policy that emphasized which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: obtaining territory to build the power of the United States
converting non-white people in other territories to Christianity
ceasing financial support to newly formed republics in Latin America and Asia
ending European colonialism in the Western Hemisphere

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/95f81ad4aa2c4556be57cfcd0f330121.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1899BalanceCartoon.jpg), 1899

**The image was created most directly in response to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the end of the Spanish-American War
the start of the Philippine-American War
the end of American occupation in former Spanish territories
the start of the practice of building up the American military

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/95f81ad4aa2c4556be57cfcd0f330121.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1899BalanceCartoon.jpg), 1899

**The image most strongly supports which of the following arguments about the Spanish-American War?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Spanish-American War helped to solidify the United States as a burgeoning world power.
The Spanish-American War left the United States weak against potential foreign threats.
The Spanish-American War benefitted the Spanish more than the United States.
The Spanish-American War ended the practice of colonialism throughout the entire world.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24d199b320ea86f70ab9896bd59a3f36.png)

>Caption: “Uncle Sam (*in his new class in Civilization*)— Now, children, you’ve got to learn these lessons whether you want to or not. But just take a look at the class ahead of you, and remember that, in a little while, you will feel as glad to be here as they are!” 

>-Source: Louis Dalrymple, "School Begins," [Wikimedia Commons,](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/e/e4/School_Begins_%28Puck_Magazine_1-25-1899%29.jpg) 1899

**The image most closely reflects which of the following popular beliefs in the United States in the early-twentieth-century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States has a duty to educate and “Christianize” the people living in the former Spanish colonies and Hawaii.
The United States should not engage in imperialism, even if it does support the economy.
The United States should support the development of democratic governments in the former Spanish colonies and Hawaii.
The United States was violating its own value of popular sovereignty by controlling Puerto Rico, Hawaii, Cuba, and the Philippines.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24d199b320ea86f70ab9896bd59a3f36.png)

>Caption: “Uncle Sam (*in his new class in Civilization*)— Now, children, you’ve got to learn these lessons whether you want to or not. But just take a look at the class ahead of you, and remember that, in a little while, you will feel as glad to be here as they are!” 

>-Source: Louis Dalrymple, "School Begins," [Wikimedia Commons,](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/e/e4/School_Begins_%28Puck_Magazine_1-25-1899%29.jpg) 1899

**Which of the following changes to the United States during the late nineteenth-century most directly contributed to the development depicted in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States started annexing overseas territories to increase trade with East Asia and Latin America.
The United States intervened in countries who had communist governments.
The United States cut itself off from the rest of the world to protect its own interests.
The United States collected money from Latin American countries in exchange for American protection.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24d199b320ea86f70ab9896bd59a3f36.png)

>Caption: “Uncle Sam (*in his new class in Civilization*)— Now, children, you’ve got to learn these lessons whether you want to or not. But just take a look at the class ahead of you, and remember that, in a little while, you will feel as glad to be here as they are!” 

>-Source: Louis Dalrymple, "School Begins," [Wikimedia Commons,](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/e/e4/School_Begins_%28Puck_Magazine_1-25-1899%29.jpg) 1899

**Which of the following developments is the most direct cause of the development expressed in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the ratification of the Treaty of Paris in 1898
the United States’ victory in the Philippine-American War
the passage of the Teller Amendment in 1898
the American entry into World War I
